summary: Rule chunk from The Rules page 83, chunk 3.

mechanics.raw_text: One memory sector is enough memory for a bot to havea sill at skill level 1. Twenty memory sectors could be filed with software for one Skill at level 20, or twenty skils at level 1, oF any other combination of skills, Usually, software is loaded into a bot, and rarely changed, However, some bots are assign- fed mem cards. A mem card is a packet of chips, about the size of a cassete, in which software s stored, The bot can plug the packet {nike loading a home video game, and load the software into its resident memory. Each mem card contains software for a specitic skill, at a specific level. For example, a card labelled “Laser Weapons 7” loads the bot with software letting i fire laser weapons at skill level 7. I the bot doesn’t have at Feast 7 memory sectors, it couldnt load the software. If it has 7/or more sector, but all were already ‘occupied by other software, it would have to erase some software to make room forthe laser weapons software ‘Note that while @ mem card can give a bot knowledge of any skill, the bot’s ability to use the skill is constrained by its physical makeup, ven if a cranebot has a memcard with Medicine 15 software, the fact that its manipulatory digits are the size of station ‘wagons might make it a litle dificult for the bot to perform microsurgery. By the way, itis completely untrue that PLC occasionally mislabels:software packages, It just never happens that a bot is loaded with ‘what PCs think is one skill, but turns out to be another one entirely.
